chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR Constitution Petition No. S-122 of 2022 DATE OF HEARING ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 1. For Orders on office objection. 2. For hearing of main case. O R D E R. 01-07-2025. Mr. Abdul Qadir Khanzada, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Agha Athar Hussain, Assistant AAG, Sindh. _____*******______ Mr. Farhan Ali Shaikh advocate files power on behalf of respondents No. 8 & 9, which is taken on record. Admittedly there is civil dispute between the parties; consequently, the petitioner approached the trial Court by filing direct complaint u/s 3 & 4 of Illegal Dispossession Act, 2005, which is pending adjudication before learned IIIrd Additional Sessions Judge Naushahro Feroze. Learned counsel for the petitioner submits that one Liaquat Ali, who is famer of respondent No. 7, has lodged FIR bearing Crime No. 15/2025, offence u/s 381-A, 511, 34 PPC at Police Station Lakha Road against some unknown persons; therefore, petitioner apprehends his malicious involvement and arrest. He further submits that private respondents in league with official respondents trying to harass the petitioner illegally. The official respondents are legally bound to act in accordance with law. Order accordingly. In support of his contention, he under the cover of statement files Photostat copy of such FIR, which is taken on record. Learned counsel for the private respondents submits that in fact there is civil dispute between the parties but neither they have harassed the petitioner nor intent to harass them in any manner. Same statements have been filed by the official respondents, which are taken on record.

Learned counsel for the private respondents submits that in fact there is civil dispute between the parties but neither they have harassed the petitioner nor intent to harass them in any manner. Same statements have been filed by the official respondents, which are taken on record. The official respondents are legally bound to act in accordance with law. Order accordingly. In view of above, the instant petition stands disposed of. J U D G E Nasim/P.A
